title: Binary search tree
prompt: A binary search tree keeps every value in order: smaller values go left, larger go right — so finding a value halves the search at every step, the same intuition as binary search. Implement BST with insert(x) and contains(x) returning True/False. Duplicates: inserting a value already present may leave the tree unchanged.

details.restate: Build a BST (binary search tree) class that keeps values in order: at every node, smaller values live down the left side and larger values down the right. It needs insert(x) and contains(x); inserting a value that's already there changes nothing.

io.because: 3 was inserted, and the tree can find it

terms.meaning: a structure where each node has at most two children, a left and a right
